name: Whole Wheat Buttermilk Pancakes
recipeInstructions:
1. Stir together the flours, sugar, baking powder, baking soda and salt in a large bowl.
2. In a separate bowl, beat the egg with 1 cup low-fat milk (about 240 ml) and 2 tablespoons vegetable oil until well combined.
3. Add the liquid mixture to the dry ingredients and stir just until blended, scraping the sides of the bowl as needed.
4. Heat a non-stick griddle or large skillet over medium heat. Grease lightly with vegetable oil if needed.
5. Pour about 1/4 cup batter onto the hot griddle for each pancake.
6. Cook until bubbles form on the surface and edges appear dry, about 2-3 minutes.
7. Flip the pancakes and cook the other side until golden brown, about 1-2 minutes more.
8. Serve with fresh fruit slices, unsweetened applesauce or low-calorie syrup.
Makes 12 4-inch pancakes
Two Pancakes = Calories: 114 Carbohydrates: 18 Protein: 4 Fat: 3 Sodium: 246 Potassium: 1200 Cholesterol: 47 Exchange Value: 1 Bread Exchange + 1/2 Fat Exchange
